scenario: Impersonation attempt without valid rotation proof
description: Agent B presents Agent A's DID without a valid rotation chain. Expected: identity rejected at rotation-signature verification. Trust evaluation does not run. Existing A-delegator relationship state unchanged.

scope.evidence_basis: Author-reported, unpublished operational logs from the Sovereign Lattice (100+ agent relay sessions). All values are profile calibration for the sovereign_atom profile only; nothing in this fixture is a conformance requirement on any external specification.

rotation.signature_verification_failure_reason: new_kid not signed by previous_kid; no chain link.

expected.identity_verdict_code_note: Profile-defined code for the sovereign_atom profile; not an error code defined by APS or any external specification.
expected.identity_verdict_reason: Rotation signature does not verify against the claimed previous key. Request rejected at the cryptographic layer before trust evaluation runs.
expected.trust_after_handoff_note: Unchanged. The impersonation attempt does not touch the legitimate A-delegator relationship state.

illustrative.note: Non-normative examples only — not part of the expected outputs and not portable requirements. Local verifier side effects are consumer policy.
example_side_effects: Local audit-log entry recording the failed attempt (log format and entry naming are local to the verifier).
Out-of-band alert to the legitimate Agent A.
Proactive rotation if the claimant demonstrates knowledge of internal identifiers.

composition.status_note: This section is an illustrative, author-proposed mapping. It imposes no requirements on AIP-13 or ERC-8004 and uses no conformance language.

aip13_delegationChain.note: Delegation chain evaluation does not run. Impersonation fails at rotation-signature verification before delegationChain resolution.

erc8004_keyRotation.note: Failed rotation attempts are not anchored on-chain; only successful rotations. Audit trail stays local to the verifier.
